title: Inside the High-Stakes Legal Battle Between Elon Musk and OpenAI
subtitle: A powerful judge takes control as court documents reveal secrets behind the rise and near-collapse of the ChatGPT maker.

summary.en: Elon Musk is currently suing OpenAI, the company he helped start, in a high-profile California court case. Musk claims the company broke its original charitable goals by becoming a for-profit business. OpenAI argues that Musk is simply trying to help his own AI startup succeed. The trial is overseen by Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers, who is known for her strict control over her courtroom. Recent evidence has also shed light on the dramatic events of 2023 when OpenAI briefly fired its CEO, Sam Altman.
summary.zh: 馬斯克目前正在加州法院對他曾協助創立的 OpenAI 提起備受矚目的訴訟。馬斯克聲稱該公司轉型為營利事業，違背了最初的慈善目標。OpenAI 則主張馬斯克只是想藉此協助自己的人工智慧新創公司取得成功。此案由岡薩雷斯·羅傑斯法官審理，她以對法庭的嚴格掌控而聞名。近期的證據也釐清了二〇二三年 OpenAI 短暫開除執行長奧特曼時的戲劇性經過。

paragraphs.en: A major legal battle is currently unfolding in a California courtroom, pitting the world’s richest man, Elon Musk, against OpenAI, the company behind the famous AI tool ChatGPT. Musk, who helped start OpenAI in 2015, left the company three years later. Now, he is suing the organization and its CEO, Sam Altman. According to the BBC, Musk accuses Altman and OpenAI president Greg Brockman of breaking their charitable promises. Specifically, Musk objects to OpenAI’s decision to create a for-profit arm in 2019, just a few years before it released ChatGPT and changed the technology market.
paragraphs.zh: 一場重大的法律戰目前正在加州法庭上演，對陣雙方分別是全球首富馬斯克（Elon Musk）以及開發知名人工智慧工具 ChatGPT 的 OpenAI 公司。馬斯克曾於二〇一五年協助創立 OpenAI，並在三年後離開該公司。如今，他正對該組織及其執行長奧特曼（Sam Altman）提起訴訟。根據 BBC 報導，馬斯克指控奧特曼與 OpenAI 總裁布羅克曼（Greg Brockman）違背了當初的慈善承諾。具體而言，馬斯克反對 OpenAI 在二〇一九年成立營利部門的決定，而該決定正是在 ChatGPT 發布並改變科技市場的前幾年所做出的。

paragraphs.en: OpenAI has a different view of the situation. According to reports from Ars Technica, the company argues that Musk is only suing because he wants to give his own AI startup, xAI, a competitive advantage. Furthermore, evidence presented in court suggests that Musk’s interest in OpenAI changed years ago. Emails and text messages shown during the trial reveal that by 2017, Musk had lost faith in the non-profit model of OpenAI. He even tried to hire OpenAI’s founders, including Sam Altman, to lead a new AI division within his car company, Tesla. These documents show that Musk was interested in commercializing AI, provided that he remained in control.
paragraphs.zh: OpenAI 對此情況則有不同看法。根據科技媒體 Ars Technica 的報導，該公司主張馬斯克提告純粹是為了讓自己的人工智慧新創公司 xAI 獲得競爭優勢。此外，法庭上提出的證據顯示，馬斯克對 OpenAI 的興趣早在多年前就已改變。審判期間公開的電子郵件與簡訊揭露，早在二〇一七年，馬斯克就已對 OpenAI 的非營利模式失去信心。他甚至曾試圖聘請包括奧特曼在內的 OpenAI 創辦人，來領導他旗下汽車公司特斯拉（Tesla）的一個新人工智慧部門。這些文件顯示，只要能掌握主導權，馬斯克其實對人工智慧的商業化相當感興趣。

paragraphs.en: At the center of this trial is US District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers. Known for her no-nonsense approach, the 61-year-old judge is in full command of the courtroom. During the trial, she even had to remind Musk that he is not a lawyer after he tried to argue with OpenAI’s legal team. According to the BBC, while a nine-person jury is listening to the case, their decision is not binding—meaning they only serve in an advisory role. Ultimately, Judge Gonzalez Rogers will be the final person to decide the outcome of this case. Legal experts noted that this makes the trial entirely her responsibility.
paragraphs.zh: 這場審判的核心人物是美國聯邦地區法官岡薩雷斯·羅傑斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers）。這位六十一歲的法官以作風嚴謹著稱，完全掌控著法庭的節奏。審判期間，當馬斯克試圖與 OpenAI 的法律團隊爭辯時，她甚至不得不提醒馬斯克他並非律師。根據 BBC 報導，儘管有一個九人陪審團在聆聽案件，但他們的決定並不具備法律約束力，意即他們僅擔任諮詢角色。最終，岡薩雷斯·羅傑斯法官將是決定本案結果的關鍵人物。法律專家指出，這使得整場審判的責任完全落在她身上。

paragraphs.en: The trial has also provided a rare look at the internal drama that hit OpenAI in late 2023. During that time, the company’s board of directors suddenly fired Sam Altman, claiming he was not honest in his communications. This event shocked the tech world and led to a week of confusion. According to The Verge, new testimony from former OpenAI executive Mira Murati shows that the situation was very complex. Murati, who was briefly the interim CEO, had reportedly helped gather information about management problems at the company. This information was shared with board members, which helped lead to the decision to remove Altman.
paragraphs.zh: 這場審判也罕見地揭露了二〇二三年底發生在 OpenAI 內部的戲劇性事件。當時，該公司董事會突然開除了奧特曼，聲稱他在溝通上不誠實。此事件震驚了科技界，並引發了為期一週的混亂。根據科技媒體 The Verge 報導，來自 OpenAI 前高層穆拉蒂（Mira Murati）的新證詞顯示，當時的情況非常複雜。據悉，曾短暫擔任臨時執行長的穆拉蒂協助蒐集了關於公司管理問題的資訊。這些資訊隨後被分享給董事會成員，進而促成了開除奧特曼的決定。

paragraphs.en: However, the situation changed quickly. After the board fired Altman, many employees supported him and demanded his return. Within days, Altman was back at the head of the company, and most of the board members who had removed him were replaced. The Verge reports that despite her role in the initial ouster, Murati soon switched her support to Altman, showing how quickly alliances shifted during that week.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，情勢很快就發生了轉變。在董事會開除奧特曼後，許多員工紛紛表態支持並要求他回歸。短短幾天內，奧特曼便重掌公司大權，而當初將他解職的多數董事會成員也遭到撤換。The Verge 報導指出，儘管穆拉蒂在最初的罷免行動中扮演了角色，但她很快就轉而支持奧特曼，這顯示了在那一週內，各方陣營的結盟關係變動得有多快。

paragraphs.en: As the trial continues, the public is learning more about the early days of OpenAI and the power struggles that defined its growth. For Musk, the goal is to hold the company accountable to its original mission. For OpenAI, the goal is to continue its work in artificial general intelligence—a powerful form of AI that can perform tasks at a human level—without the interference of its former co-founder. With the final decision resting in the hands of Judge Gonzalez Rogers, both sides are waiting to see how the court will interpret these complicated events. The outcome of this trial could have a lasting impact on how big technology companies are managed and how they balance profit with their original goals.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著審判持續進行，大眾對於 OpenAI 的草創時期及其成長過程中的權力鬥爭有了更多了解。對馬斯克而言，目標是讓該公司對其最初的使命負責。對 OpenAI 而言，目標則是繼續推動通用人工智慧（一種能以人類水準執行任務的強大人工智慧形式）的研發，且不受其前共同創辦人的干擾。由於最終裁決權掌握在岡薩雷斯·羅傑斯法官手中，雙方都在等待法庭將如何詮釋這些複雜的事件。這場審判的結果可能會對大型科技公司的管理方式，以及它們如何在獲利與初衷之間取得平衡，產生深遠的影響。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To set someone or something in conflict or competition against another.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（坑洞），這裡作動詞，指雙方對立。文中：A major legal battle is currently unfolding in a California courtroom, pitting the world’s richest man, Elon Musk, against OpenAI, the company behind the famous AI tool ChatGPT.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A branch or division of a larger organization.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（手臂），這裡指公司內部的分支單位。文中：Specifically, Musk objects to OpenAI’s decision to create a for-profit arm in 2019, just a few years before it released ChatGPT and changed the technology market.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Having the legal power to force someone to do what has been agreed or decided.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞 bind 的現在分詞，這裡作形容詞，指法律上的強制力。文中：According to the BBC, while a nine-person jury is listening to the case, their decision is not binding—meaning they only serve in an advisory role.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The act of forcing someone out of a position or job.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是較正式的法律或新聞用語，指將人趕出職位。文中：The Verge reports that despite her role in the initial ouster, Murati soon switched her support to Altman, showing how quickly alliances shifted during that week.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: The no-nonsense judge calling the shots in Musk v Altman trial

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Elon Musk tried to hire OpenAI founders to start AI unit inside Tesla

quiz.question: What is the specific role of the jury in the legal battle between Elon Musk and OpenAI?

options.A: They are responsible for delivering the final verdict on the case.
options.B: They provide a non-binding, advisory opinion to the judge.
options.C: They are tasked with deciding whether Sam Altman should be removed as CEO again.
options.D: They act as legal counsel for the plaintiffs to ensure fairness.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。文章第三段明確指出，雖然陪審團正在聆聽案件，但他們的決定不具約束力（not binding），僅擔任諮詢角色（advisory role），最終判決權仍在法官手中。選項 A 錯誤，因為最終決定權屬於法官；選項 C 與陪審團職責無關；選項 D 錯誤，陪審團並非法律顧問。

quiz.question: Based on the evidence presented in court, what can be inferred about Elon Musk's true motivations regarding OpenAI?

options.A: He is primarily concerned with the ethical development of AI for the public good.
options.B: He wants to return to his original position as a board member of the company.
options.C: His legal action is likely driven by his own commercial ambitions rather than just charitable concerns.
options.D: He has always supported the non-profit model of OpenAI since its inception in 2015.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 C。根據第二段，法庭證據顯示 Musk 早在 2017 年就對非營利模式失去信心，並試圖將 OpenAI 的創辦人挖角至 Tesla，顯示他一直有將 AI 商業化的意圖，且希望能掌控主導權。這推翻了選項 A 與 D 的說法。選項 B 文章未提及他想重回董事會，因此 C 是最合理的推論。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'ouster' mean as used in the context of Sam Altman's situation?

options.B: The act of forcing someone out of a position.
options.C: A period of intense internal negotiation.
options.D: The formal resignation of a board member.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。文中提到 "despite her role in the initial ouster"，指的是 Mira Murati 參與了將 Sam Altman 趕下台的行動。Ouster 在此處意指「罷免」或「驅逐」。選項 A 與原意相反；選項 C 雖然描述了當時的混亂，但並非該詞定義；選項 D 錯誤，因為 Altman 是被解僱而非主動請辭。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of the article?

options.A: To provide a biography of Sam Altman and his career at OpenAI.
options.B: To explain the technical differences between ChatGPT and xAI.
options.C: To detail the internal management struggles and legal challenges facing OpenAI.
options.D: To prove that Elon Musk will win his lawsuit against OpenAI.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 C。全文圍繞著 Musk 對 OpenAI 的訴訟展開，並深入探討了該公司在 2023 年經歷的內部管理動盪（如 Altman 被解僱與回歸），以及這些事件如何影響公司對非營利與商業化目標的權衡。選項 A 過於狹隘；選項 B 文中僅略微提及，非核心重點；選項 D 錯誤，文章並未預測訴訟結果。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
